title: Anal itching

examination.text: Supports documenting onset, duration, localisation, generalisation, time course, intensity, triggers, associated symptoms, medicines, exposures, personal and family history, whole skin, scalp, nails, oral and anogenital examination, and primary versus secondary lesions.
examination.rationale: The exact chronic-pruritus section supports onset, duration, localisation, time course, intensity, triggers, associated symptoms, medicines, exposures, personal and family history, and clinician-performed anogenital examination without assigning a cause.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting onset, duration, localisation, generalisation, time course, intensity, triggers, associated symptoms, medicines, exposures, personal and family history, whole skin, scalp, nails, oral and anogenital examination, and primary versus secondary lesions.

source.title: European Guideline on Chronic Pruritus: In cooperation with the European Dermatology Forum (EDF)

source_limitations: Specific anorectal symptoms, sexual or safeguarding context, consent, chaperone, examination, infection, cause, test, treatment, referral, and urgency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
